---
title: q.label Roadmap
---

Open work for future-aware target construction. The candidates below draw from
methods Marcos López de Prado developed, co-developed, or popularized in his
financial-machine-learning research.

## Event discovery

- [ ] Structural-break event filters: Chu-Stinchcombe-White CUSUM, Chow-type
  Dickey-Fuller, and SADF/explosiveness tests
- [ ] Volatility-normalized CUSUM thresholds with explicit lagging rules so an
  event threshold uses only information available at that timestamp
- [ ] Information-driven event times from tick, volume, and dollar imbalance
  bars and run bars; bar construction belongs in `q.data`, while `q.label`
  consumes the resulting event index
- [ ] Event-filter diagnostics comparing clock-time sampling with
  information-driven sampling, including event density and regime stability

## Label construction

- [ ] Optional high/low intrabar barrier touches with an explicit same-bar tie
  policy
- [ ] Event-specific asymmetric barriers and dynamic vertical horizons derived
  from point-in-time volatility or market regimes
- [ ] Optimal profit-taking and stop-loss calibration for mean-reverting and
  momentum processes without brute-force backtest parameter searches
- [ ] Trend-scanning extensions for irregular timestamps, session-aware
  horizons, robust regression, and multiscale label consensus
- [x] Rare-label pruning with transparent minimum-frequency rules and a report
  of discarded observations
- [ ] A first-class meta-labeling workflow that joins primary-model side,
  predicted probability, barrier outcome, and secondary binary target without
  losing event-lifetime metadata
- [ ] Label-stability surfaces across barrier multipliers, horizons, and target
  widths to reveal targets that depend on a narrow parameter choice

## Uniqueness and sample weights

- [x] Sparse indicator-matrix construction showing which observations belong
  to each event lifetime
- [x] Sequential bootstrap sampling based on average uniqueness, with a seeded
  and reproducible draw API
- [x] Linear time-decay weights that preserve a configurable fraction of older
  observations instead of silently deleting them
- [ ] Piecewise and nonlinear time-decay schedules
- [x] Composable weights combining average uniqueness, concurrency-attributed
  absolute returns, time decay, and optional class balancing
- [ ] Uniqueness-aware train/bag sampling adapters for `q.model`, while keeping
  estimator implementations outside `q.label`

## Diagnostics and scale

- [ ] Label-distribution, barrier-touch, event-lifetime, concurrency, and
  uniqueness diagnostics under `q.plot`
- [x] Purging and embargo metadata export for `q.model.selection`, including
  the maximum label end time required to prevent overlapping train/test paths
- [ ] Synthetic Ornstein-Uhlenbeck, momentum, and regime-switching paths for
  measuring label recovery, stability, and class balance against known dynamics
- [ ] Scalable chunked execution and sparse interval operations for very large
  event sets
